title: The Anvil at the Fire
text: Serah spends the grey hour drilling the newest recruits herself, correcting grips with two fingers and no words. When one boy drops his shield a third time she picks it up, hands it back, and stays until he holds it right. 'The line is the line,' she says, and leaves.

title: The Bridge Story Again
text: Kael holds the fire's whole circle with the story of the bridge at Veld — eleven riders, the gate, the fall of the portcullis an arm's length behind him. It gets better every telling. He watches your tent while he tells it.

title: The Count Is the Count
text: Mother Rooke catches the salt-merchant's thumb on the scale before the bag touches the board. She does not raise her voice. The merchant repacks every bag while she watches, and throws in a tenth one free.

title: Twice Burned
text: A picket died of fever in the night. Before anyone thought to ask, Brother Hale had built the pyre and lit it, singing the dawn office alone in the dark. 'Once for safety,' he says when you come to watch. 'Once for spite.'

title: The Same Mule Twice
text: Vex has, by all accounts, sold the same grey mule to two different tinkers and refereed the resulting dispute for a fee. The company is five silver richer and the tinkers have jointly sworn never to deal with him again. He seems pleased with all outcomes.

title: Blood by the Picket Line
text: Two soldiers settle a dice grudge with knives before the sergeants pull them apart. One won't march for a week; his opponent won't march again. Quiet camp after.

title: The Bones Come Up Wrong
text: A traveling gambler takes a week's pay off half a squad before someone checks the dice. The dice were honest — which somehow makes it worse. Grumbling in the ranks about coin.

title: Glass on the Wind
text: A black wind off the Fields drives glass-dust through camp for a day and a night. Everything glitters and everyone bleeds a little. The old hands tie rags over their faces and say nothing; the new blood learns.

title: One Empty Bedroll
text: A soldier from the third file slipped the pickets before dawn, taking his boots and leaving his back-pay. The sergeants ask whether to chase him. The old hands already know the answer.

title: A Good Night
text: Someone found a cask, someone found a fiddle, and for one evening the Fields sound like somewhere people live. Even the sentries are humming. The company remembers what it's for.

title: Glass Wolves at the Pickets
text: Lean shapes with mirror-shard eyes test the picket line in the dark. The watch drives them off with torches, but not before they drag down two of the horses and a man who went to cut them loose.

title: Salvage Luck
text: A forage party turns up a seam of grave-glass where the storm stripped the topsoil — small pieces, but Ossian's factor pays on the spot and asks no questions about the plot markers.

title: Thin Purses
text: The pay-line was quiet this week, which is how soldiers are loudest. Around the fires they are counting on their fingers what the treasury owes and what it holds. The sergeants suggest, respectfully, that the count should improve.

title: The Flood Ditch
text: A night flood off the glass hills nearly takes the east tents; officers and soldiers dig the ditch together in the rain until dawn. Bad night, good story. The kind the company keeps.

title: A Gift from the Bone Court
text: A pale legate leaves a lacquered case at the camp's edge: bonewrought mail, sized — unsettlingly — to your shoulders, and a note in a fine dead hand: 'The Court notices quality.'

title: The Throne's Sweetener
text: A fly-crowned herald delivers a chest of mixed coin that smells faintly of the grave, and a message spoken in a borrowed voice: 'The Throne feeds those who feed it.'

title: The Court Remembers
text: For services rendered, the Bone Court sends a case of black-glass arrowheads and a writ of passage sealed in wax the color of old teeth. The legate's note: 'Continue.'

title: The Throne Provides
text: The Throne's gratitude arrives on a wagon: salt-meat (you check; it's pig), barrels of dark beer, and a purse. The herald waits to watch you accept it, which is the actual gift being purchased.

title: The Legate Grows Pointed
text: The pale legate arrives unannounced and inventories your camp with its eyes. 'The Court dislikes shared instruments,' it says. 'Choose a hand to be held by. Soon.'

title: The Herald Asks Plainly
text: The fly-crowned herald does not sit. 'The Throne hears you sell to the bone-men too. The Throne is patient. The Throne is not infinitely patient. Decide what you are.'

title: Struck from the Court's Ledgers
text: A single rider in bone-lacquer delivers a strip of vellum: your company's name, written and then struck through. No other message. None needed.

title: The Throne Marks You
text: In the night someone — something — plants a spear at your gate with a dead crow lashed to it, facing your tent. The old hands don't touch it. The Throne's opinion, delivered.

title: A Stranger in Grey
text: A stranger in grey shared Kael's fire last night — travel-stained, well-spoken, gone by morning watch. Nobody heard the conversation. Kael hasn't mentioned it.

title: A Letter Under the Saddle
text: A groom found a letter sewn under Kael's saddle-flap and — being paid to notice things — mentioned it to Rooke's people before returning it unread. Good paper. A banner-captain's seal, though whose banner he couldn't say.

title: A Broker's Proposition
text: One of Ossian's rivals has been drinking with Vex — twice this week — and paying. The word 'consignment' was overheard, and the phrase 'nobody counts glass in a war.'

title: Pilgrims on the Road
text: A ragged column of sun-order pilgrims passed within sight of camp, bound for the shrine country, singing the dawn office. Brother Hale stood at the pickets and watched them out of sight. He has not spoken since.

title: The Anvil Writes a Letter
text: Serah sat up past the late watch writing something by candle, sealing it, and locking it in her campaign chest. She has never once, in anyone's memory, written a letter.

title: Talk of Other Banners
text: There is talk at the fires of other companies wintering fat off richer wars — the kind of talk that is always around, and only sometimes means something.

title: The Fields Tighten
text: Both powers have stopped courting and started counting. Contracts now come with conditions, envoys with ledgers. On the Fields, the space between the two armies is getting narrower — and the company is standing in it.

title: The Glass Runs Toward an Edge
text: Every scout, trader, and drunk agrees on one thing now: this war has a shape, and the shape has an ending in it. The powers are done maneuvering. What remains is the arithmetic of the field.

title: The Roads Fill
text: A column of refugees passes the camp from dawn to dusk — carts, dogs, grandmothers, everything a village is when you pour it onto a road. They do not ask for help. They have stopped asking anyone.

title: What the Storm Uncovered
text: The glass-wind stripped a hillside two leagues east and uncovered a battle nobody remembers — hundreds of dead in a fashion of armor no living smith makes. By evening, both powers had riders circling it. The Fields have been feeding this war a long time.

title: The Dead Walk the Flats
text: Travelers off {flats} report the unbannered dead walking in numbers again — drawn, the glasspickers say, to wherever the war is about to go next. The glasspickers are usually right.
